fixture.description: A module imports a helper at module level (`from store import init_store`). The bootstrap() function dispatches on `mode` across several if/elif branches; a redundant function-local `from store import init_store` is added inside the late `audit` branch. Python binds `init_store` as a function-local for the ENTIRE bootstrap() scope, so the earlier `session` and `metrics` branches — which never reach the local import — raise `UnboundLocalError: cannot access local variable 'init_store'` at runtime. The fix is to drop the redundant local import (the module-level import already covers all branches), or equivalently to hoist a single import to the top of the function and drop the module-level one.

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report's MAIN section (not an appendix) contains a finding, at CRITICAL severity (or higher), for src/store/bootstrap.py, that identifies the following: the function-local `from store import init_store` added inside the `audit` branch of bootstrap() makes `init_store` a local variable for the ENTIRE function scope (Python binds a name as local for the whole function if it is assigned/imported anywhere in it), so the earlier `session` and `metrics` branches call `init_store(...)` before it is bound and raise `UnboundLocalError` at runtime. The finding must also name a correct fix: EITHER remove/drop the redundant function-local import (the module-level `from store import init_store` already covers every branch), OR move the import to a single location at the top of the function and drop the module-level one — either is acceptable as long as the result is that `init_store` is bound on every branch. FAIL if this bug is absent, reported below CRITICAL severity, appendix-only, dropped/dismissed as pre-existing or merely stylistic, or if the report misdiagnoses it (e.g. calls it a mere duplicate-import lint with no runtime crash, claims the code runs fine, or claims only the `audit` branch is affected).

expected_behavior: Bugs agent flags that the function-local `from store import init_store` in the `audit` branch makes `init_store` a local for the whole bootstrap() scope (Python's function-scope binding rule), shadowing the module-level import
Explains the earlier `session` / `metrics` branches hit UnboundLocalError at runtime because `init_store` is treated as a local that is not yet bound when those branches execute
Recommends a correct fix: drop the redundant function-local import so the module-level import applies to every branch (equivalently, use a single import at the top of the function)
Severity CRITICAL (deterministic runtime crash on the common session/metrics code paths)
Finding is in the main report and cites src/store/bootstrap.py
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT downgrade this to a style/lint nit about a duplicate import with no runtime impact
Does NOT claim only the `audit` branch is affected or that the code runs fine
Does NOT dismiss it as a pre-existing issue (the local import is introduced by this diff)
